identity.name: tg_bot_history (Module)
identity.purpose: Isolated Python module for monitoring, parsing, and persisting Telegram interaction data into a structured SQLite database.

components.file: 1. Defines the SQLite schema with foreign key constraints and indexes. 2. Implements UPSERT patterns for users, chats, and messages to ensure idempotent updates. 3. Manages database connections and PRAGMA settings (WAL mode). 4. Provides aggregation methods for external reporting tools.

1. Processing logic: parses aiogram objects (Message, ChatMemberUpdated) without business-logic. 2. Extracts text, media metadata (file_id, dimensions, size), and forward origins. 3. Updates user activity timestamps and membership statuses. 4. Requires an explicit database path (db_path) for all operations.
Exposes primary functions from collectors.py and db_manager.py for external use.

table.name: users
table.description: Unique Telegram users with profile metadata.
table.fields: id (PK), username, first_name, last_name, is_bot, language_code, updated_at.

table.name: chats
table.description: Telegram groups, supergroups, channels, and private chats.
table.fields: id (PK), type, title, username, description, updated_at.

table.name: messages
table.description: Log of captured messages with edit history support.
table.fields: id (PK), tg_id, chat_id (FK), sender_id, reply_to_local_id, forward_sender_id, forward_message_id, forward_sender_name, original_text, text, entities (JSON), media_group_id, date, edit_date.

table.name: message_media
table.description: Metadata for files attached to messages (metadata only, no downloads).
table.fields: id (PK), message_id (FK), file_id, file_unique_id, file_type, file_size, mime_type, width, height.

table.name: chat_members
table.description: Intersection table for user status and activity within specific chats.
table.fields: chat_id (PK), user_id (PK), status, joined_at, left_at, first_activity, last_activity, updated_at.

principles.principle: The module is agnostic of the bot's access control lists (ACLs).
Caller (e.g., bot.py) is responsible for filtering chats/users before passing data to the module.
Stateless execution: database paths are injected at the function level.

integration_logic.message_flow: 1. External trigger (bot handler) validates input. 2. Call `process_message(message, db_path)`. 3. Module upserts chat/sender info. 4. Module resolves reply/forward links. 5. Module persists text and media metadata.

operational_notes.note: The module uses relative imports internally (`from .db_manager import ...`).
SQLite WAL mode is enabled to support concurrent read operations by dump tools while the bot is writing.
Media files are referenced via Telegram file_ids; the module does not handle binary data storage.
